title: ficus carica var. johannis
intro: Ficus carica var. johannis, also known as the edible fig or black mission fig, is a variety of the common fig tree (Ficus carica) that is widely cultivated for its delicious fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to help alleviate various health issues such as digestive problems, diarrhea, and respiratory infections. The fruit, leaves, and roots of the Ficus carica var. johannis plant have been used in herbal preparations to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, wounds, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or topical application to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
